research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: triage nurse
stage_or_process_step: nurse triage
topic_category: red flag missed; normal-test trap; incomplete history
finding: Triage is a rapid risk sort, not a diagnosis; subtle chronic or complex symptoms may appear low acuity if vitals are normal.
what_happened: Classify acuity using ESI: immediate life-saving intervention, high risk/severe pain, then expected resources, with danger-zone vitals review.
what_was_identified: Identified: patients needing immediate lifesaving intervention and high-risk care; risk: triage depends on nurse pattern recognition and local resource assumptions.
what_was_missed: Identified: patients needing immediate lifesaving intervention and high-risk care; risk: triage depends on nurse pattern recognition and local resource assumptions.
cause_or_contributing_factor: red flag missed; normal-test trap; incomplete history
product_or_system_implication: Separate emergency risk score from diagnostic uncertainty; capture clinician concern and prior visits even when ESI level is lower.
notes: ESI is a documented front-door workflow; its resource prediction step can under-prioritize problems that need diagnostic synthesis rather than many ED resources.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: ED clinicians, radiology, consultants
stage_or_process_step: first clinician assessment; imaging/lab interpretation; discharge
topic_category: red flag missed; repeated visits ignored; no follow-up ownership
condition_or_issue: stroke, MI, aortic dissection, VTE, meningitis/encephalitis, sepsis, spinal cord injury/compression, lung cancer
finding: High-risk diagnoses may be missed at the first visit and only recognized after repeat ED return or deterioration.
what_happened: Systematic review estimated diagnostic error harms and common high-harm conditions.
what_was_identified: Missed/delayed: serious ED diagnoses, especially vascular, neurologic, infection and cancer presentations.
what_was_missed: Missed/delayed: serious ED diagnoses, especially vascular, neurologic, infection and cancer presentations.
cause_or_contributing_factor: red flag missed; repeated visits ignored; no follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Flag high-harm conditions linked to symptom clusters and require explicit exclusion/safety-netting before discharge.
notes: Useful as cross-setting failure evidence rather than a local workflow manual.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: registered clinician / senior nurse / RAT team
stage_or_process_step: arrival; registration; nurse triage; streaming; first clinician assessment
topic_category: poor handoff; red flag missed; no follow-up ownership
finding: Patients may experience an 'occult wait' before triage if registration queues or digital check-in create hidden delays.
what_happened: Initial assessment can include streaming, triage and rapid assessment/treatment to identify sickest patients and direct others to right service.
what_was_identified: Identified: sickest patients and appropriate service route; risk: redirection decisions can become unsafe if not clinically governed.
what_was_missed: Identified: sickest patients and appropriate service route; risk: redirection decisions can become unsafe if not clinically governed.
cause_or_contributing_factor: poor handoff; red flag missed; no follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Track real elapsed time from physical arrival to clinical assessment, not just time after registration.
notes: Represents a national ED front-door flow rather than a diagnostic guideline.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: registered clinician
stage_or_process_step: registration; initial assessment; vitals
topic_category: arrival; registration; red flag missed; poor handoff
finding: Operational queues can hide clinical risk before the patient is visible to the triage system.
what_happened: Register quickly, perform initial assessment by trained registered clinician, record minimum clinical fields and use early warning scores where relevant.
what_was_identified: Identified: front-door risk; missed risk if queues are unmanaged before registration/assessment.
what_was_missed: Identified: front-door risk; missed risk if queues are unmanaged before registration/assessment.
cause_or_contributing_factor: arrival; registration; red flag missed; poor handoff
product_or_system_implication: Use queue surveillance and arrival-time capture; include deterioration prompts before formal triage is complete.
notes: Supports the black-zone concept of pre-triage waits.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: front desk, triage RN, intake RN, ED provider, lab/radiology
stage_or_process_step: arrival; intake; labs ordered; imaging ordered; lab interpretation; discharge/admission
topic_category: documentation error; no one connected the dots; normal-test trap
finding: Decision support needs upstream data completeness and downstream reassessment when diagnosis does not fit.
what_happened: Patient arrives by EMS/walk-in; triage/intake RN collects key data; provider orders labs/CXR; CURB-65 and clinical assessment guide admission vs outpatient antibiotics.
what_was_identified: Identified: pneumonia severity and disposition; risk: pneumonia mimic or alternative diagnosis may be missed if CDS anchors on CAP.
what_was_missed: Identified: pneumonia severity and disposition; risk: pneumonia mimic or alternative diagnosis may be missed if CDS anchors on CAP.
cause_or_contributing_factor: documentation error; no one connected the dots; normal-test trap
product_or_system_implication: Expose missing CURB-65 elements and create 'pursue other diagnoses' tracking, not just CAP disposition.
notes: Real implementation workflow shows how data from different roles enters a decision point.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: patient, digital triage system, streaming clinician, receiving service
stage_or_process_step: arrival; intake form; triage; referral
topic_category: digital access gap; incomplete history; red flag missed
finding: Self-service can speed flow but may miss nuance, language barriers or worsening while waiting.
what_happened: Patient self-checks in on kiosk/tablet, completes symptom triage, and can be prioritised, redirected or referred through service routing.
what_was_identified: Identified: rapid symptom-led prioritisation and redirection; risk: digital access, literacy and symptom-description gaps.
what_was_missed: Identified: rapid symptom-led prioritisation and redirection; risk: digital access, literacy and symptom-description gaps.
cause_or_contributing_factor: digital access gap; incomplete history; red flag missed
product_or_system_implication: Add accessibility fallback, clinician override, real-time deterioration alerts and visibility of prior visits.
notes: A current example of digital triage embedded in urgent/emergency care.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: UTC multidisciplinary staff, nurse, clinician, GP/ED/specialist pathways
stage_or_process_step: arrival; first clinician assessment; labs ordered; imaging ordered; referral; follow-up
topic_category: wrong specialty; poor handoff; no follow-up ownership
condition_or_issue: minor injuries/ailments and urgent non-life-threatening problems
finding: UTC boundaries are porous; patients may need escalation, diagnostics or follow-up ownership outside the UTC.
what_happened: UTCs accept booked and walk-in patients; provide basic diagnostics or protocols; share records/post-event messages and refer to ED/specialists when needed.
what_was_identified: Identified: conditions manageable outside ED; risk: lack of diagnostic capability or wrong redirection.
what_was_missed: Identified: conditions manageable outside ED; risk: lack of diagnostic capability or wrong redirection.
cause_or_contributing_factor: wrong specialty; poor handoff; no follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Show available diagnostics, escalation criteria and post-visit ownership in the same workflow.
notes: UTC standard describes setting-level capabilities and data-sharing requirements.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: reception, nurse, UTC clinician
stage_or_process_step: arrival; vitals; first clinician assessment; discharge/referral
topic_category: red flag missed; patient self-advocacy required
finding: The workflow depends on patients knowing when and how to report deterioration while waiting.
what_happened: On arrival, patients may have observations by nurse, may wait by acuity not arrival order, and can be assessed/treated onsite or referred; GP is updated afterwards.
what_was_identified: Identified: worsening symptoms during wait if patient tells staff; risk: passive reliance on patient to report deterioration.
what_was_missed: Identified: worsening symptoms during wait if patient tells staff; risk: passive reliance on patient to report deterioration.
cause_or_contributing_factor: red flag missed; patient self-advocacy required
product_or_system_implication: Provide waiting-room check-ins and explicit 'tell us now if...' symptom prompts in multiple languages.
notes: Patient-facing description of the real UTC arrival process.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: triage/rooming staff, clinician
stage_or_process_step: vitals; nurse triage; first clinician assessment
topic_category: normal-test trap; lab trend missed; documentation error
finding: Vitals create early signal but can be incomplete, delayed or not trended against prior visits.
what_happened: Initial vitals tailored to age, complaint and status; abnormal vitals reported and repeated.
what_was_identified: Identified: physiologic instability; missed: serious disease with normal or incomplete vitals.
what_was_missed: Identified: physiologic instability; missed: serious disease with normal or incomplete vitals.
cause_or_contributing_factor: normal-test trap; lab trend missed; documentation error
product_or_system_implication: Require abnormal-vital acknowledgment, repeat-vital timers and trend comparison across visits.
notes: Practical urgent-care policy source, not a population study.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: registration/intake staff, clinician
stage_or_process_step: registration; intake form; nurse triage
topic_category: red flag missed; incomplete history; poor handoff
condition_or_issue: emergency red-flag presentations presenting to urgent care
finding: Non-clinical intake staff may be the first safety barrier.
what_happened: Registration/intake staff ask reason for visit; red flags trigger immediate rooming and clinician involvement or ED transfer.
what_was_identified: Identified: conditions too high-risk for routine urgent care; missed: red flags hidden by vague complaint or poor intake phrasing.
what_was_missed: Identified: conditions too high-risk for routine urgent care; missed: red flags hidden by vague complaint or poor intake phrasing.
cause_or_contributing_factor: red flag missed; incomplete history; poor handoff
product_or_system_implication: Use plain-language red-flag checklist and escalation button at registration.
notes: Shows how triage starts before formal clinician assessment.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: patient, NHS 111 digital algorithm, callback clinician/receiving service
stage_or_process_step: before visit; intake form; referral
topic_category: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
finding: Digital triage may fragment complex histories into a single main symptom.
what_happened: Patient completes one symptom pathway; service gives advice such as self-care, pharmacist, GP, UTC, ED or callback.
what_was_identified: Identified: service disposition; missed: multi-symptom or known-condition context if algorithm is used one symptom at a time.
what_was_missed: Identified: service disposition; missed: multi-symptom or known-condition context if algorithm is used one symptom at a time.
cause_or_contributing_factor: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
product_or_system_implication: Allow multi-symptom summaries, 'not captured' text, prior-risk integration and easy escalation to human review.
notes: Patient-facing NHS 111 online flow.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: patient, NHS Pathways, service directory, nurse/clinician callback
stage_or_process_step: before visit; intake form; referral
topic_category: wrong specialty; poor handoff; fragmented care
finding: The handoff is only as good as the service directory and referral visibility.
what_happened: Uses structured triage and service directory to recommend ambulance/ED/UTC/out-of-hours GP/GP/pharmacist/self-care outcomes.
what_was_identified: Identified: appropriate service destination; missed: directory availability or local pathway mismatches can redirect incorrectly.
what_was_missed: Identified: appropriate service destination; missed: directory availability or local pathway mismatches can redirect incorrectly.
cause_or_contributing_factor: wrong specialty; poor handoff; fragmented care
product_or_system_implication: Audit outcome-to-arrival completion and failed referral attempts; surface no-capacity conditions.
notes: Staff-facing service information documents integration pieces.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: patient, online form, admin/care navigator, GP/nurse
stage_or_process_step: before visit; intake form; first clinician assessment; follow-up
topic_category: digital access gap; incomplete history; no follow-up ownership; chronic illness invalidation
condition_or_issue: varied primary-care requests; hidden serious illness
finding: Online access can create a digital bottleneck and reduce the chance to observe/examine patients.
what_happened: Asynchronous online consultations support clinical/admin requests but may be capped or rerouted by practices for safety.
what_was_identified: Missed/delayed: safety incidents when digital tools reduce access, distort history or weaken proactive/long-term care.
what_was_missed: Missed/delayed: safety incidents when digital tools reduce access, distort history or weaken proactive/long-term care.
cause_or_contributing_factor: digital access gap; incomplete history; no follow-up ownership; chronic illness invalidation
product_or_system_implication: Create safe fallback channels, complexity flags, remote-to-face-to-face conversion rules and continuity prompts.
notes: HSSIB source includes patient and staff focus groups and practice visits.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: telehealth clinician, patient/caregiver
stage_or_process_step: medical history; physical exam; vitals; referral; follow-up
topic_category: normal-test trap; incomplete physical exam; poor follow-up ownership
condition_or_issue: acute or chronic problems assessed remotely
finding: Telehealth changes the signal set available for diagnosis.
what_happened: Telehealth can be safer when embedded in a medical home with records, tests, referrals and follow-up; risk rises when exam and communication are limited.
what_was_identified: Missed: diagnostic cues requiring auscultation, private/inaccessible body areas, objective vitals or hands-on exam.
what_was_missed: Missed: diagnostic cues requiring auscultation, private/inaccessible body areas, objective vitals or hands-on exam.
cause_or_contributing_factor: normal-test trap; incomplete physical exam; poor follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Decision support should classify problems as telehealth-suitable vs requiring in-person exam/labs/imaging.
notes: Useful cross-setting telehealth safety evidence.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: reception/admin/care navigator, GP, nurse, pharmacist, allied professional
stage_or_process_step: before visit; intake form; care navigation; first clinician assessment
topic_category: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
finding: Routing can optimize capacity but also become a diagnostic gate if symptoms are under-described.
what_happened: Modern access model directs patients via phone/online/in-person to the right professional first time, using care navigation and demand/capacity management.
what_was_identified: Identified: routing need; missed: clinical complexity if navigation lacks enough symptom/risk context.
what_was_missed: Identified: routing need; missed: clinical complexity if navigation lacks enough symptom/risk context.
cause_or_contributing_factor: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
product_or_system_implication: Separate admin routing from clinical triage; capture escalation flags and repeated contacts.
notes: National primary-care access policy.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: trained receptionist/admin/care coordinator, nurse/GP for clinical triage
stage_or_process_step: care navigation; clinical triage; referral
topic_category: poor handoff; symptom dismissed; red flag missed
finding: Ambiguity over whether a contact is administrative or clinical can lose risk signals.
what_happened: Care navigation signposts to appropriate clinician or service; clinical triage by nurse/GP makes early clinical decisions.
what_was_identified: Identified: difference between signposting and clinical triage; missed: escalation if administrative navigation substitutes for clinical judgment.
what_was_missed: Identified: difference between signposting and clinical triage; missed: escalation if administrative navigation substitutes for clinical judgment.
cause_or_contributing_factor: poor handoff; symptom dismissed; red flag missed
product_or_system_implication: Make role boundaries explicit and route uncertain cases to clinical triage.
notes: Professional guidance clarifies first-contact roles.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: GP/primary-care clinician, specialist service
stage_or_process_step: medical history; physical exam; labs ordered; referral; safety netting
topic_category: normal-test trap; referral delay; rare disease delay
finding: Threshold pathways may leave low-risk but persistent symptoms in a monitoring black zone.
what_happened: Guideline organizes referral/investigation actions by cancer site, symptom and findings; includes safety netting and diagnostic-process advice.
what_was_identified: Identified: cancer suspicion thresholds; missed: patients below threshold or with nonspecific/vague symptoms unless active monitoring occurs.
what_was_missed: Identified: cancer suspicion thresholds; missed: patients below threshold or with nonspecific/vague symptoms unless active monitoring occurs.
cause_or_contributing_factor: normal-test trap; referral delay; rare disease delay
product_or_system_implication: Add active-monitoring tasks and symptom persistence triggers, not only referral thresholds.
notes: A key formal workflow source for primary-care suspicion/referral.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: GP/primary-care team, patient
stage_or_process_step: follow-up; repeat visit; diagnosis discussion
topic_category: normal-test trap; no follow-up ownership; repeated visits ignored
condition_or_issue: possible cancer or other unresolved condition
finding: A negative test can prematurely close a diagnostic episode.
what_happened: Safety-netting gives time-bound follow-up, documents advice, ensures tests/referrals completed and monitors persistent symptoms even after negative tests.
what_was_identified: Identified: ongoing risk despite low-risk or negative initial results; missed: no owner for follow-up tasks.
what_was_missed: Identified: ongoing risk despite low-risk or negative initial results; missed: no owner for follow-up tasks.
cause_or_contributing_factor: normal-test trap; no follow-up ownership; repeated visits ignored
product_or_system_implication: Build safety-net plans with due dates, owner, patient-facing instructions and re-presentation counters.
notes: Clear source for the normal-test trap and repeat-presentation workflows.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: GP, nurse, admin staff, external provider
stage_or_process_step: labs ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up
topic_category: abnormal result not followed up; documentation error; no follow-up ownership
finding: Test-result management crosses many people and organisations.
what_happened: Practice needs systems for ordering, tracking attendance, return/non-return, review, filing, patient communication and abnormal follow-up.
what_was_identified: Missed: abnormal result not acted on, result not returned, no patient communication or clinician absence gap.
what_was_missed: Missed: abnormal result not acted on, result not returned, no patient communication or clinician absence gap.
cause_or_contributing_factor: abnormal result not followed up; documentation error; no follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Implement closed-loop result tracking, unresolved-result inboxes and backup reviewer rules.
notes: Regulator source supports result-management black zones.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: ED, named GP, physiotherapist, out-of-hours GPs, multiple practice GPs, nurse, oncology
stage_or_process_step: repeat visit; medical history; specialist review; emergency escalation
topic_category: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; missing family/history context; repeated visits ignored
condition_or_issue: metastatic breast cancer presenting as back pain
finding: No one clinician/system connected the repeated back-pain presentations with prior cancer risk.
what_happened: Over 8 months the patient saw many clinicians; eventual GP found spinal lump and sent patient to ED where CT found metastatic cancer.
what_was_identified: Missed/delayed: unresolved symptoms and cancer-history context were not surfaced across repeat contacts.
what_was_missed: Missed/delayed: unresolved symptoms and cancer-history context were not surfaced across repeat contacts.
cause_or_contributing_factor: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; missing family/history context; repeated visits ignored
product_or_system_implication: Surface unresolved symptom threads, prior high-risk history and multiple-clinician patterns in the EHR.
notes: Named public case from national safety investigation; includes patient voice.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: GP team; peer reviewer; specialist input
stage_or_process_step: repeat visit; physical exam; referral; labs ordered
topic_category: repeated visits ignored; no one connected the dots; bias or minimization
condition_or_issue: persistent or escalating unexplained symptoms; cancer in origin story
finding: Repeat visits are often visible only as isolated encounters.
what_happened: After three presentations, GP teams are prompted to reflect, review and rethink, consider face-to-face exam, second opinion, tests or referral.
what_was_identified: Identified: repeat presentation as a diagnostic signal; missed: if repeat visits are not counted across clinicians/channels.
what_was_missed: Identified: repeat presentation as a diagnostic signal; missed: if repeat visits are not counted across clinicians/channels.
cause_or_contributing_factor: repeated visits ignored; no one connected the dots; bias or minimization
product_or_system_implication: Create automatic repeat-presentation counters across phone/online/in-person and linked symptoms.
notes: Current NHS initiative; useful product pattern for persistent symptoms.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: GPs, A&E staff, gynaecologists, patient
stage_or_process_step: medical history; referral; specialist review; repeat visit
topic_category: symptom dismissed; chronic illness invalidation; bias or minimization; patient self-advocacy required
finding: Chronic pain and women's health symptoms can be minimized despite repeated presentations.
what_happened: Survey documented repeated GP visits and A&E attendance before diagnosis; respondents reported dismissal/minimisation.
what_was_identified: Missed/delayed: endometriosis diagnosis; patients often required repeated self-advocacy.
what_was_missed: Missed/delayed: endometriosis diagnosis; patients often required repeated self-advocacy.
cause_or_contributing_factor: symptom dismissed; chronic illness invalidation; bias or minimization; patient self-advocacy required
product_or_system_implication: Track symptom duration/severity, repeated visits and invalidating language; prompt specialist referral criteria review.
notes: Advocacy survey gives patient voice and quantitative delay data.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: GP, hospital outpatient service, patient
stage_or_process_step: referral; follow-up; lab interpretation
topic_category: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; patient self-advocacy required
finding: Referral is not a completed action unless the patient is seen, tests are done and results acted on.
what_happened: Healthwatch gathered patient reports of waiting after GP referral, confusion over results and pressure to chase.
what_was_identified: Missed/delayed: follow-up appointments, specialist action, scans/tests; GP sometimes unable to accelerate despite abnormal tests.
what_was_missed: Missed/delayed: follow-up appointments, specialist action, scans/tests; GP sometimes unable to accelerate despite abnormal tests.
cause_or_contributing_factor: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; patient self-advocacy required
product_or_system_implication: Track referral state, wait risk, abnormal-test escalation and patient-visible next step.
notes: Patient voice source on referral/follow-up gaps.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: radiologist, requesting clinician, ED/hospital team, patient/family
stage_or_process_step: imaging ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up; emergency return
topic_category: abnormal result not followed up; red flag missed; repeated visits ignored
finding: Imaging workflows fail at multiple points: ordering, performance, interpretation, communication, follow-up.
what_happened: Ombudsman reported repeated failures in scan reading, carrying out scans and follow-up; a cancer case was only identified at a fifth A&E visit after lesion not reported.
what_was_identified: Missed/delayed: abnormal scan findings and cancer diagnosis.
what_was_missed: Missed/delayed: abnormal scan findings and cancer diagnosis.
cause_or_contributing_factor: abnormal result not followed up; red flag missed; repeated visits ignored
outcome_or_consequence: example six-week delay; other cases vary
product_or_system_implication: Closed-loop imaging: critical finding acknowledgment, patient notification and escalation if no action documented.
notes: Patient-complaint/ombudsman evidence with public family quote.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: radiologist, ED clinician, GP, patient
stage_or_process_step: imaging ordered; imaging interpretation; discharge; follow-up
topic_category: poor handoff; abnormal result not followed up; no follow-up ownership
condition_or_issue: lung cancer / unexpected significant radiology finding
finding: Responsibility can diffuse between radiology, ED, GP and patient.
what_happened: Investigation traced multistep communication of unexpected significant findings and weak assurance that action occurred.
what_was_identified: Missed/delayed: significant finding not received or acted on; read receipts not enough.
what_was_missed: Missed/delayed: significant finding not received or acted on; read receipts not enough.
cause_or_contributing_factor: poor handoff; abnormal result not followed up; no follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Require an accountable result owner, escalation timer and auditable action completion, not just report delivery.
notes: Important source for test-result black zones beyond labs.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: hospital clinicians, diagnostic stewardship team, lab/radiology, outpatient follow-up team
stage_or_process_step: discharge; follow-up; imaging/lab interpretation
topic_category: poor handoff; no follow-up ownership; abnormal result not followed up
condition_or_issue: missed diagnoses after discharge; lung malignancy suspicion; pending tests
finding: Discharge can sever ownership of pending results.
what_happened: Hospital diagnostic excellence framework encourages targeting pending tests and suspicious imaging follow-up.
what_was_identified: Identified: high-risk diagnostic follow-up areas; missed: patients leaving hospital before test closure.
what_was_missed: Identified: high-risk diagnostic follow-up areas; missed: patients leaving hospital before test closure.
cause_or_contributing_factor: poor handoff; no follow-up ownership; abnormal result not followed up
product_or_system_implication: Create discharge-pending-test registry and patient/clinician notification loops.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: referrer, provider triage clinician/admin, patient
stage_or_process_step: referral; specialist review; follow-up
topic_category: wrong specialty; referral delay; poor handoff
condition_or_issue: conditions needing specialist opinion or diagnostic pathway
finding: Referral triage creates a new work queue where responsibility can be unclear to patient and referrer.
what_happened: Referral Assessment Services allow provider review before booking; outcomes include booking, redirecting, requesting more info, or returning advice.

what_was_missed: Identified: suitable pathway before appointment; missed: wrong worklist/status or insufficient referral information delays care.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: referrer, provider clinician, diagnostic service
stage_or_process_step: referral; imaging ordered; labs ordered; specialist review
topic_category: incomplete history; wrong specialty; documentation error
condition_or_issue: conditions suitable for direct-to-test or clinical assessment
finding: Straight-to-test pathways are efficient only when referral information is complete.
what_happened: Diagnostic e-RS models include direct-to-test, clinical assessment then test, and one-stop consultant-led models.
what_was_identified: Identified: bypassing first outpatient appointment for needed tests; missed: poor referral templates can omit critical context.
what_was_missed: Identified: bypassing first outpatient appointment for needed tests; missed: poor referral templates can omit critical context.
cause_or_contributing_factor: incomplete history; wrong specialty; documentation error
product_or_system_implication: Embed mandatory clinical context fields and feedback loops for rejected/inappropriate tests.
notes: Operational source on outpatient diagnostics.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: GP/referrer, specialist consultant/team
stage_or_process_step: referral; specialist review; imaging/labs ordered; follow-up
topic_category: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; normal-test trap
finding: Advice can reduce unnecessary appointments but must not become a soft rejection of unresolved symptoms.

what_was_identified: Identified: faster specialist input; missed: advice-only management without clear follow-up if symptoms persist.
what_was_missed: Identified: faster specialist input; missed: advice-only management without clear follow-up if symptoms persist.

outcome_or_consequence: case example: new patients 2-4 weeks vs 12-14 weeks before
product_or_system_implication: Record advice decisions with safety-net tasks and escalation criteria.
notes: Includes clinician voice and pathway redesign example.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: health center staff, hospital/ED, primary-care clinician
stage_or_process_step: referral; discharge; follow-up
topic_category: poor handoff; fragmented care; no follow-up ownership
condition_or_issue: post-ED/hospital follow-up and ongoing primary-care needs
finding: Safety-net clinics may be responsible for follow-up despite care occurring elsewhere.
what_happened: Health centers must have referral/hospital arrangements and processes to receive/record ED or hospital visit information and follow-up actions.
what_was_identified: Identified: need for continuity; missed: discharge info not received or tracked.
what_was_missed: Identified: need for continuity; missed: discharge info not received or tracked.
cause_or_contributing_factor: poor handoff; fragmented care; no follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Ingest outside discharge/test data and assign follow-up ownership automatically.
notes: Federal compliance source for FQHC workflow expectations.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_missed: Identified: coordination model; missed: PCMH standards may not guarantee specific diagnostic follow-up closure without local systems.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_identified: Identified: abnormal FIT needing colonoscopy; missed: patients not reached or declining navigation remain at risk.
what_was_missed: Identified: abnormal FIT needing colonoscopy; missed: patients not reached or declining navigation remain at risk.
cause_or_contributing_factor: abnormal result not followed up; patient self-advocacy required; no follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Track abnormal-screen closure and navigator barrier categories to completion.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

stage_or_process_step: public health screening; referral; follow-up
topic_category: referral delay; fragmented care; documentation error

what_was_identified: Identified: implementation barriers to closing abnormal FIT follow-up.
what_was_missed: unknown
cause_or_contributing_factor: referral delay; fragmented care; documentation error
product_or_system_implication: Build navigator work queues, cross-organization data sharing and colonoscopy-status reconciliation.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: screening programme, lab, specialist nurse, colonoscopy service, GP/patient
stage_or_process_step: before visit; public health screening; lab interpretation; referral; follow-up
topic_category: normal-test trap; no follow-up ownership
finding: Screening can create false reassurance if symptoms are separated from screening result interpretation.
what_happened: Eligible people receive FIT at home; results usually within 2 weeks; further tests lead to specialist nurse and usually colonoscopy; negative screen does not rule out cancer if symptoms occur.
what_was_identified: Identified: occult blood needing diagnostic work-up; missed: symptomatic people reassured by negative screening result.
what_was_missed: Identified: occult blood needing diagnostic work-up; missed: symptomatic people reassured by negative screening result.
cause_or_contributing_factor: normal-test trap; no follow-up ownership
product_or_system_implication: Pair negative results with symptom-trigger advice and GP follow-up prompts.
notes: Patient-facing pathway; strong flow map source.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: screening office, radiographers, radiologists, assessment clinic, MDT
stage_or_process_step: before visit; public health screening; imaging ordered; imaging interpretation; specialist review
topic_category: no follow-up ownership; abnormal result not followed up; patient self-advocacy required

what_was_missed: Identified: mammographic abnormalities; missed: non-attendance, unclear images, communication failures.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_missed: Identified: access barriers; missed: patients who disappear between positive screen and diagnostic test.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_missed: Identified: hidden NCD/cancer risk; missed: follow-up/compliance if referral loop is not closed.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

stage_or_process_step: medical history; physical exam; referral; emergency escalation

finding: Low-resource workflows depend heavily on algorithms and referral availability.

what_was_identified: Identified: severe illness and common syndromes without diagnostics; missed: atypical illness not covered by algorithm or supply/referral gaps.
what_was_missed: Identified: severe illness and common syndromes without diagnostics; missed: atypical illness not covered by algorithm or supply/referral gaps.
cause_or_contributing_factor: red flag missed; referral delay; incomplete history

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

condition_or_issue: malaria, pneumonia, diarrhoea in children under five

what_was_missed: Identified: common child illnesses outside facilities; missed: severe cases if CHW stockout, training gaps or referral failure.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_missed: Identified: millions of common illness episodes; missed: sustainability, supervision and integration challenges.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: OPD nurses, clinicians
stage_or_process_step: arrival; nurse triage; vitals; first clinician assessment
topic_category: red flag missed; arrival; poor handoff
condition_or_issue: urgent/emergency cases among general OPD patients
finding: In many rural OPDs, primary and emergency care share a front door with no formal triage.
what_happened: Rural OPD implemented IITT-adapted structured triage using a three-colour checklist on charts and short training; patient flow improved without added resources.
what_was_identified: Identified: urgent/emergency cases earlier; missed: undertriage still possible and formal triage requires staff buy-in.
what_was_missed: Identified: urgent/emergency cases earlier; missed: undertriage still possible and formal triage requires staff buy-in.
cause_or_contributing_factor: red flag missed; arrival; poor handoff
outcome_or_consequence: time to provider reduced 23 minutes; total OPD stay reduced 35 minutes
product_or_system_implication: Low-cost triage checklists can be embedded into paper charts and later digitized.
notes: Real-world QI study in rural Uganda.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_missed: Identified: hidden needs and chronic disease adherence issues; missed: signals may be lost if CHW observations do not reach nurse/physician workflow.
cause_or_contributing_factor: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; documentation error
product_or_system_implication: Create structured CHW observation fields and escalation pathways into primary-care team huddles.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_missed: Identified: metabolic risk; missed: people outside eligibility/attendance or follow-up lifestyle support.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_missed: Identified: screen-positive or suspected disease; missed: non-attendance, inaccurate results if pre-test instructions not followed, confirmation follow-up gap.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

topic_category: rare disease delay; referral delay; no one connected the dots

finding: Countries need a route for serious nonspecific symptoms, not only organ-specific red flags.
what_happened: Three-legged strategy: urgent alarm symptoms to organ-specific pathways; serious nonspecific symptoms to tests and diagnostic centres; vague low-risk symptoms managed by GP with diagnostic access.
what_was_identified: Identified: cancers without alarm symptoms; missed: vague symptoms if GP access to diagnostics is limited or safety-netting weak.
what_was_missed: Identified: cancers without alarm symptoms; missed: vague symptoms if GP access to diagnostics is limited or safety-netting weak.

outcome_or_consequence: reported diagnosis wait reductions in case-study evidence
product_or_system_implication: Create nonspecific-symptom pathways with GP gut-feeling field, blood-panel bundle and diagnostic-centre referral.
notes: Health-system case study of a distinctive diagnostic pathway model.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_happened: Cohort study of urgent referral to Denmark's non-organ-specific cancer pathway; around one fifth had cancer.
what_was_identified: Identified: substantial cancer prevalence among non-specific symptom referrals; missed: patients with serious symptoms outside pathway criteria.
what_was_missed: Identified: substantial cancer prevalence among non-specific symptom referrals; missed: patients with serious symptoms outside pathway criteria.
cause_or_contributing_factor: rare disease delay; referral delay; normal-test trap
product_or_system_implication: Support structured referral for uncertainty, not just classic red flags.
notes: Peer-reviewed evidence for nonspecific symptom diagnostic pathways.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

what_was_missed: Identified: strong longitudinal data infrastructure; missed: data availability does not guarantee synthesis of trends or patient narrative.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
person_or_group_involved: referring physician, collection site staff, lab/radiology specialists, patient

what_was_missed: Identified: lab/imaging workflow and result dissemination; missed: action on result still depends on clinician/patient follow-up loop.

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues

research_topic: Real health-system patient flows for identifying health issues
